speaker: LUCIUS
text: Who, the Lord Timon? He is my very good

speaker: LUCIUS
text: friend and an honorable gentleman.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: We know him for no less, though we

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: are but strangers to him. But I can tell you one

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: thing, my lord, and which I hear from common

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: rumors: now Lord Timon’s happy hours are done

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: and past, and his estate shrinks from him.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Fie, no, do not believe it. He cannot want for

speaker: LUCIUS
text: money.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: But believe you this, my lord, that

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: not long ago one of his men was with the Lord

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: Lucullus to borrow fifty talents, nay, urged

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: extremely for ’t, and showed what necessity

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: belonged to ’t, and yet was denied.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: How?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: I tell you, denied, my lord.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: What a strange case was that! Now, before the

speaker: LUCIUS
text: gods, I am ashamed on ’t. Denied that honorable

speaker: LUCIUS
text: man? There was very little honor showed in ’t. For

speaker: LUCIUS
text: my own part, I must needs confess I have received

speaker: LUCIUS
text: some small kindnesses from him, as money, plate,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: jewels, and suchlike trifles, nothing comparing to

speaker: LUCIUS
text: his; yet had he mistook him and sent to me, I

speaker: LUCIUS
text: should ne’er have denied his occasion fifty talents.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: See, by good hap, yonder’s my lord.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text:
I have sweat to see his Honor.
                 
                To
                 
                Lucius
                .
               My

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: honored lord.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Servilius. You are kindly met, sir. Fare thee

speaker: LUCIUS
text: well. Commend me to thy honorable virtuous lord,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: my very exquisite friend.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: May it please your Honor, my lord hath

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: sent—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Ha! What has he sent? I am so much endeared

speaker: LUCIUS
text: to that lord; he’s ever sending. How shall I thank

speaker: LUCIUS
text: him, think’st thou? And what has he sent now?

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: Has only sent his present occasion now, my

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: lord, requesting your Lordship to supply his

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: instant use with fifty talents.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I know his Lordship is but merry with me.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: He cannot want fifty-five hundred talents.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: But in the meantime he wants less, my lord.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: If his occasion were not virtuous,

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: I should not urge it half so faithfully.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Dost thou speak seriously, Servilius?

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: Upon my soul, ’tis true, sir.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: What a wicked beast was I to disfurnish

speaker: LUCIUS
text: myself against such a good time, when I might ha’

speaker: LUCIUS
text: shown myself honorable! How unluckily it happened

speaker: LUCIUS
text: that I should purchase the day before for a

speaker: LUCIUS
text: little part, and undo a great deal of honor! Servilius,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: now before the gods, I am not able to do—the

speaker: LUCIUS
text: more beast, I say!—I was sending to use Lord

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Timon myself, these gentlemen can witness; but I

speaker: LUCIUS
text: would not for the wealth of Athens I had done ’t

speaker: LUCIUS
text: now. Commend me bountifully to his good Lordship,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: and I hope his Honor will conceive the fairest

speaker: LUCIUS
text: of me, because I have no power to be kind. And tell

speaker: LUCIUS
text: him this from me: I count it one of my greatest

speaker: LUCIUS
text: afflictions, say, that I cannot pleasure such an honorable

speaker: LUCIUS
text: gentleman. Good Servilius, will you

speaker: LUCIUS
text: befriend me so far as to use mine own words to

speaker: LUCIUS
text: him?

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: Yes, sir, I shall.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I’ll look you out a good turn, Servilius.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: True, as you said, Timon is shrunk indeed,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And he that’s once denied will hardly speed.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Do you observe this, Hostilius?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              STRANGER
text: Ay, too well.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Why, this is the world’s soul, and just of the same piece

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Is every flatterer’s sport. Who can call him his friend

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: That dips in the same dish? For, in my knowing,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Timon has been this lord’s father

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: And kept his credit with his purse,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Supported his estate, nay, Timon’s money

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Has paid his men their wages. He ne’er drinks

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: But Timon’s silver treads upon his lip.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: And yet—O, see the monstrousness of man

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: When he looks out in an ungrateful shape!—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: He does deny him, in respect of his,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: What charitable men afford to beggars.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              STRANGER
text: Religion groans at it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: For mine own part,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: I never tasted Timon in my life,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Nor came any of his bounties over me

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: To mark me for his friend. Yet I protest,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: For his right noble mind, illustrious virtue,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: And honorable carriage,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Had his necessity made use of me,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: I would have put my wealth into donation,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: And the best half should have returned to him,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: So much I love his heart. But I perceive

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: Men must learn now with pity to dispense,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              STRANGER
text: For policy sits above conscience.
